bio: Johnes Mwashushe Mwaruma is a Kenyan politician representing Taita-Taveta County in the Senate as a member of the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), a seat he holds in the 13th Parliament. Within the Senate he serves on the Committee on Education, the Committee on Lands, Environment and Natural Resources, and the County Public Accounts Committee, giving him a role in shaping policy on schooling, natural resource management, and county-level financial oversight. His work centers on representing the interests of Taita-Taveta County at the national level, engaging with issues ranging from land use and environmental protection to public education and accountability in county government spending.

Jones Mwagogo Mlolwa is a Kenyan politician who represented Voi Constituency in Taita-Taveta County as a Member of Parliament for the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) across the 11th and 12th Parliaments. He first won the seat in 2013, unseating incumbent Danson Mwazo Mwakulegwa, and served on the Departmental Committee on Finance, Planning and Trade. A vocal advocate for coastal interests, he pushed in 2014 for a Coastal Political Party to strengthen the region's bargaining power with the national government, and in 2016 he was elected secretary of the New Coast Parliamentary Group. He lost his re-election bid in 2017 and made another unsuccessful attempt to reclaim the Voi seat in 2022, narrowly losing to Khamis Chome by fewer than 1,000 votes.

Joseph H. Maero Oyula is a Kenyan politician serving as Member of the National Assembly for Butula Constituency in Busia County, representing the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM). He was first elected in 2017 and was re-elected for a second term in the 2022 general election, currently sitting in the 13th Parliament (2022-2027). In the House he serves on the Departmental Committee on Finance and National Planning as well as the National Government Constituencies Development Fund Committee, roles through which he engages with national budgeting and the allocation of constituency development resources for Butula.

Joseph Kimutai Cherorot is a Kenyan politician representing Kipkelion East Constituency in Kericho County as a Member of the National Assembly, elected on the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket for the 13th Parliament (2022-2027). In the House he serves on the Departmental Committee on Housing, Urban Planning and Public Works, and the Special Funds Account Committee, positions that put him at the center of debates on infrastructure development and public housing policy as well as oversight of specialized public funds. His legislative work focuses on advancing the interests of Kipkelion East constituents in matters of urban planning, road and building infrastructure, and government-administered special funds.

Joseph Kipkoross Makilap is a Kenyan politician who represents Baringo North Constituency as a Member of the National Assembly, elected on the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket for the 13th Parliament (2022-2027). Within the House he sits on the Departmental Committee on Education and the Public Debt and Privatisation Committee, and has taken part in joint sittings of National Assembly departmental committees. Through these roles he engages with policy on the education sector as well as oversight of Kenya's public debt levels and the privatisation of state assets, work that shapes his contribution to national fiscal and education debates on behalf of his Baringo North constituents.

Major-General Joseph Kasaine Ole Nkaissery was a Kenyan politician and retired military officer who represented Kajiado Central Constituency in the National Assembly, first elected on a KANU ticket in the 9th Parliament and later re-elected as a member of the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) in the 11th Parliament. He served as Member of Parliament from 2002 until 2014, when he resigned his seat to take up appointment as Cabinet Secretary for Internal Security and Coordination of National Government. Beyond politics, he was recognized as a senior elder of the Maasai community. Nkaissery died suddenly on 8 July 2017 while still serving as Cabinet Secretary, closing out a career that spanned both the military and national politics.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Internal Security and Coordination of National Government

bio: Joseph Samal Lomwa is a Kenyan politician representing Isiolo North Constituency as a Member of the National Assembly. He first won the seat in 2013 on a United Republican Party (URP) ticket, succeeding Mohammed Kuti, and served on the Public Works, Roads and Transport Committee during the 11th Parliament. He sat out the 2017 election after failing to secure the backing of his clan's council of elders, but returned to contest and win the seat again in 2022, this time on a Jubilee Party ticket, polling 10,326 votes to defeat opponents Hassan Odha and Mwenda Thuranira. In the 13th Parliament (2022-2027) he serves on the Labour Committee and the National Cohesion and Equal Opportunity Committee, focusing on labour rights and social cohesion alongside his constituency representation.

Joseph Wathigo Manje is a Kenyan politician, teacher, and entrepreneur who represented Kajiado North Constituency as a Member of Parliament, first elected on a TNA ticket in the 11th Parliament and re-elected under the Jubilee Party banner in the 12th Parliament. Before entering elective politics, he worked as a teacher with Kenya's Teachers Service Commission (TSC) and later served as a TSC Commissioner. He also served as the Electoral Commission of Kenya's (ECK) South Rift Presidential Election Zonal Coordinator. Alongside his public service, Manje built business interests in the leather and construction industries, an entrepreneurial streak that has run alongside his teaching and political careers.

professional.title: South Rift Presidential Election Zonal Coordinator

bio: Joshua Aduma Owuor is a Kenyan lawyer and politician who has represented Nyakach Constituency in Kisumu County as a Member of the National Assembly for the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) since 2013. Now serving his third term after re-election in 2017 and 2022, he sits in the 13th Parliament on the National Government Constituencies Development Fund (NG-CDF) Committee and the Administration and Internal Security Committee. His long tenure representing Nyakach reflects sustained voter confidence, and his legal background informs his work on committees overseeing constituency development funding and matters of internal security and public administration.

Joshua Chepyegon Kandie is a Kenyan politician representing Baringo Central Constituency as a Member of the National Assembly. He was first elected to the seat in 2017 on a Maendeleo Chap Chap Party ticket, and was re-elected in 2022 after switching to the United Democratic Alliance (UDA). In the 13th Parliament (2022-2027) he serves on the Defence, Intelligence and Foreign Relations Committee and the Public Petitions Committee, having previously taken part in departmental committee work during the 12th Parliament. His committee assignments place him at the intersection of national security oversight and constituent grievance resolution, complementing his role representing Baringo Central.

Joshua Orwa Ojode was a Kenyan politician who represented Ndhiwa Constituency in the National Assembly, first appointed to Parliament on 28 June 1994 on the National Development Party ticket and later sitting in the 9th Parliament under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC). In 2007 he moved to the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM). Beyond his constituency role, he served in government as Assistant Minister for Provincial Administration and Internal Security, a position that placed him at the center of Kenya's internal security and local administration policy. His career reflects a shift across several political parties while maintaining his hold on the Ndhiwa seat and building a growing profile in national government.

professional.title: Assistant Minister for Provincial Administration and Internal Security

bio: Joyce Akai Emanikor is a Kenyan politician who has served as Woman Representative for Turkana County in the National Assembly across two terms, first elected in the 11th Parliament on a United Republican Party (URP) ticket and re-elected in the 12th Parliament under the Jubilee Party. Her tenure has centered on advocating for Turkana County, one of Kenya's more sparsely served regions, within national policy discussions. As Woman Representative, her role has included championing the interests of women and vulnerable communities in Turkana across two consecutive terms in the House, giving the county a consistent voice on the national stage.

Joyce Chepkoech Korir is a Kenyan politician whose career spans local and national government. She made history as the first female mayor of Bomet Municipality, serving in that role from 2007 to 2013, before being elected Woman Representative for Bomet County in the 12th Parliament (2017-2022) under the Jubilee Party, where she took part in departmental committee work. She was later nominated to the Senate on a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket for the 13th Parliament, continuing a long record of public service that runs from grassroots municipal leadership to national legislative office.

Joyce Kamene Kasimbi is a Kenyan politician serving as Woman Representative for Machakos County in the National Assembly under the Wiper Democratic Movement (WDM-K). First elected in 2017, she was re-elected in the 2022 general election with roughly 253,113 votes, about 66 percent of the vote, retaining the seat into the 13th Parliament (2022-2027). In the House she sits on the Departmental Committee on Trade, Industry and Cooperatives, the Constitution and Mediation Committee, and the Delegated Legislation Committee, positions through which she engages with legislative oversight of subsidiary law, constitutional matters, and trade policy alongside her advocacy for Machakos County residents.

Joyce Atieno Osogo Bensuda is a Kenyan politician representing Homa Bay County as Woman Representative in the National Assembly, elected on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket in the 2022 general election. In the 13th Parliament (2022-2027) she serves on the Blue Economy, Water and Irrigation Committee and the Communication, Information and Innovation Committee, engaging with policy on the county's fisheries and water resources as well as the broader information and communications sector. As Woman Representative, she carries the responsibility of championing the interests of Homa Bay's women and communities in national legislative debate.

Julius Kibiwott Melly is a Kenyan politician who has represented Tinderet Constituency in the National Assembly since 2013, re-elected in 2017 and 2022 and now serving his third term, having moved from the United Republican Party to the Jubilee Party and then to the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) across successive elections. Before entering politics, he was principal of Meteitei Boys Secondary School. In Parliament he has chaired the Departmental Committee on Education and also sits on the Budget and Appropriations Committee, the Liaison Committee, and the Selection Committee, giving him significant influence over national education policy and House business in the 13th Parliament (2022-2027).

Recha Julius Murgor is a Kenyan politician affiliated with the United Democratic Alliance (UDA). He began his parliamentary career by winning the Kapenguria Constituency seat in the National Assembly in the 2007 general election, before later moving to the Senate, where he was elected the third Senator for West Pokot County in 2017. He continues to represent West Pokot County in the Senate in the 13th Parliament, bringing more than a decade of legislative experience spanning both chambers of Kenya's Parliament and reflecting sustained support from West Pokot voters across different levels of representation.

Julius Kariuki Ndegwa is a Kenyan politician who represented Lamu West Constituency in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament. He was elected on the ticket of the Kenya National Congress party, with the backing of the Eagle Coalition, bringing a smaller-party voice to the representation of Lamu West's constituents in the House. His single term in office placed him among the smaller-party legislators who contested and won seats in the 2013 general election, a period marked by shifting coalition alignments across Kenya's coastal and northern constituencies.

Julius Lekakeny Sunkuli is a Kenyan politician representing Kilgoris Constituency in Narok County as a Member of the National Assembly, elected on 8 August 2022 on a Kenya African National Union (KANU) ticket for the 13th Parliament (2022-2027). Before returning to elective politics, he served as a Cabinet minister in the Kenyan government. In the House he sits on the Health Committee, the Delegated Legislation Committee, and the Selection Committee, roles that draw on his earlier cabinet experience as he engages with national health policy and legislative oversight on behalf of Kilgoris constituents.

Junet Sheikh Nuh Mohamed is a Kenyan politician who has represented Suna East Constituency in the National Assembly for the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) across the 11th, 12th, and 13th Parliaments. He currently serves as Minority Leader of the National Assembly in the 13th Parliament (2022-2027), a position that places him at the forefront of opposition strategy and House business. His committee record includes membership on the Appointments Committee, the Budget and Appropriations Committee, the House Business Committee, the Public Accounts Committee, and the Selection Committee, alongside earlier service on a Joint Select Committee during the 11th Parliament, reflecting a long-standing role in shaping parliamentary proceedings.

Kabando wa Kabando is a Kenyan politician and public affairs activist who represented Mukurweini Constituency in the National Assembly for two terms, first elected in 2007 on a Safina Party ticket, a party he remained a member of until 2022, and later sitting in the 11th Parliament as a member of The National Alliance (TNA). Outside politics, he founded the Nairobi Water Company, serving as its founding chairman, and earlier founded the Students' Opinion (STOP) lobby that helped revive the Students' Organisation of Nairobi University (SONU), where he was elected president in 1992. A prolific author and media commentator on public affairs both locally and globally, he was voted the most progressive public interest legislator in the Kenyan Parliament in 2014.

Jane Wangechi Kagiri is a Kenyan politician representing Laikipia County as Woman Representative in the National Assembly, elected on a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket in the 2022 general election. In the 13th Parliament (2022-2027) she serves on the Budget and Appropriations Committee and the Departmental Committee on Housing, Urban Planning and Public Works, giving her a voice in national budget allocation as well as housing and infrastructure policy. As Woman Representative, she is tasked with championing the interests of Laikipia's women and communities within these legislative processes, bringing constituency concerns directly into national fiscal and housing debates.

Stephen Kalonzo Musyoka is a Kenyan politician who represented Mwingi North Constituency in the National Assembly under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) and went on to serve as the tenth Vice-President of Kenya from 2008 to 2013. Under President Daniel arap Moi, he held a succession of senior posts including Secretary of the Kenya African National Union (KANU) party from 1980 to 1988, Assistant Minister for Works from 1986 to 1988, Deputy Speaker of the National Assembly from 1988 to 1992, Minister for Foreign Affairs from 1993 to 1998, and Minister of Education from 1998 to 2001. He later returned to the Foreign Affairs docket from 2003 to 2004 and served as Minister of the Environment from 2004 to 2005 under President Mwai Kibaki. After an unsuccessful bid for the presidency in 2007, he was appointed Vice-President by Kibaki in January 2008.

Kathuri Murungi is a Kenyan politician who currently represents Meru County in the Senate as a member of the United Democratic Alliance (UDA), also serving as Deputy Speaker of the Senate. He previously sat in the National Assembly for South Imenti Constituency, first elected under The National Alliance (TNA) in the 11th Parliament and later returned as an independent candidate in the 12th Parliament, where he took part in Special Funds Account committee work. In the 13th Parliament he has been ranked by Politrack Africa as the second-best-performing senator, with a rating of 72.8 percent, reflecting an active record of engagement across both the National Assembly and the Senate.

Kawira Mwangaza Mwenda is a Kenyan politician who served as Women's Representative for Meru County in the 12th Parliament, elected as an independent candidate. She went on to become the third Governor of Meru County, holding office from August 2022 until she was impeached by the Meru County Assembly on 21 August 2024 over allegations of abuse of office and incompetence. Despite a vigorous defense mounted by her legal team before the Senate, the impeachment was upheld, and the High Court of Kenya confirmed her removal from office on 14 March 2025. Her deputy, Isaac Mutuma, was sworn in as the fourth Governor of Meru County on 17 March 2025, closing out a tenure marked by sustained political controversy.

Khatib Abdalla Mwashetani is a Kenyan politician who represented Lunga Lunga Constituency in Kwale County as a Member of Parliament across two terms. He was first elected in the 2013 general election as a member of the Forum for the Restoration of Democracy-Kenya (FORD-Kenya), and was re-elected in 2017 under the Jubilee Party banner, serving in the 12th Parliament on delegated departmental committee work. His decade in the National Assembly reflected the shifting party alliances common among coastal politicians navigating Kenya's evolving political landscape over that period.

Anthony Kimani Ichung'wah is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kikuyu Constituency in Kiambu County across three Parliaments, moving from The National Alliance (TNA) in the 11th Parliament to the Jubilee Party in the 12th and the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) in the 13th. He currently serves as Leader of the Majority Party in the National Assembly during the 13th Parliament (2022-2027). His extensive committee record includes the Budget and Appropriations Committee, the Appointments Committee, the House Business Committee, the Liaison Committee, the Selection Committee, the Justice and Legal Affairs Committee, and involvement in the 2024 Finance Bill process, along with earlier service on the Mediation Committee and the Public Investments Committee, reflecting a central role in shaping the National Assembly's legislative agenda.

Boniface Kinoti Gatobu is a Kenyan politician who represented Buuri Constituency in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament, having won the seat as an independent candidate in the 2013 general election. Running without the backing of a major political party, he secured Buuri's representation in the House through direct appeal to voters rather than party machinery, a path shared by a number of independent legislators elected that year. His single term in Parliament placed him among the constituency representatives shaping the early years of Kenya's devolved constitutional order following the 2010 constitution.

Onesimus Kipchumba Murkomen is a Kenyan lawyer and politician who has represented Elgeyo-Marakwet County as Senator across three election cycles, holding the seat in the 13th Parliament as a member of the United Democratic Alliance (UDA). An Advocate of the High Court of Kenya, he has moved between the Senate and the Cabinet, having served as Cabinet Secretary for Roads, Transport and Public Works, then as Cabinet Secretary for Youth Affairs, the Creative Economy and Sports, before his current appointment as Cabinet Secretary for Interior and National Administration. His career illustrates a close interplay between legislative representation of Elgeyo-Marakwet and senior executive responsibility in national government.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Roads, Transport and Public Works
Cabinet Secretary for Youth Affairs, Creative Economy and Sports
Cabinet Secretary for Interior and National Administration

bio: Kivutha Kibwana is a Kenyan politician, legal scholar, and human rights activist who represented Makueni Constituency in the National Assembly under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC). He served in the Kenyan Cabinet as Minister for Defence and later as Minister for Environment, and was also an advisor to President Mwai Kibaki. Kibwana went on to become the first Governor of Makueni County, completing two terms in that office before his tenure ended in 2022. His career spans academia, human rights advocacy, national government, and county leadership, reflecting a consistent focus on public service and governance reform.

professional.title: Advisor to the President
professional.institution: Office of the President of Kenya

bio: Ahmed Maalim Hassan Alio is a Kenyan politician who has represented Banissa Constituency in the National Assembly since 2017, re-elected in 2022. He was first elected on an Economic Freedom Party ticket in the 12th Parliament before moving to the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) for the 13th Parliament (2022-2027). In the House he serves on a Departmental Committee, the Public Investments Committee, and the Regional Development Committee, positions through which he engages with oversight of public enterprises and development planning for Kenya's regions alongside his representation of Banissa constituents.

Lawrence Mpuru Aburi is a Kenyan politician representing Tigania East Constituency in Meru County in the National Assembly. He was first elected on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket in the 11th Parliament, and returned to the seat in the 2022 general election as leader of the National Ordinary People Empowerment Union (NOPEU), the only candidate from that party elected to the National Assembly in the 13th Parliament (2022-2027). In the House he serves on the Departmental Committee on Agriculture and Livestock, the Constitutional Implementation Oversight Committee, and the Procedure and House Rules Committee, engaging with agricultural policy, constitutional implementation, and parliamentary procedure on behalf of Tigania East.

Joseph Lemasolai Lekuton is a Kenyan politician who has represented Laisamis Constituency in Marsabit County across five Parliaments spanning more than two decades. First elected to the National Assembly in the 2007 general election on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket, he was re-elected in 2013, lost his seat in 2017, and returned to Parliament in the 2022 general election on a United Democratic Movement (UDM) ticket within the Kenya Kwanza coalition, winning with 6,157 votes after nearly a decade away from the House. In the 13th Parliament (2022-2027) he serves as Vice-Chair of the Committee on Parliamentary Powers and Privileges and also sits on the Budget and Appropriations Committee and the Liaison Committee, drawing on one of the longest legislative careers among his peers to shape House business.

Lilian Cheptoo Tomitom is a Kenyan politician who served as Woman Representative for West Pokot County in the National Assembly during the 12th Parliament, having been elected on a Jubilee Party ticket. In that role she represented the interests of West Pokot's women and communities in national legislative debate, serving alongside other county Woman Representatives elected in the same 2017 election cycle to bring gender-focused representation into the House. Her single term placed her among the cohort of legislators serving the pastoralist counties of Kenya's Rift Valley region.

Lilian Achieng Gogo is a Kenyan politician who has represented Rangwe Constituency in the National Assembly for the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) across the 12th and 13th Parliaments. In the 13th Parliament (2022-2027) she serves on the Budget and Appropriations Committee, the Procedure and House Rules Committee, and the Public Investments Committee, giving her a role in national budget allocation, House procedural matters, and oversight of state-owned enterprises. Her continued re-election reflects sustained support from Rangwe voters across successive electoral cycles and a growing legislative portfolio in the House.

Linet Chepkorir Toto is a Kenyan politician representing Bomet County as Woman Representative in the National Assembly, elected on a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket following the 9 August 2022 general election. She is presumed to be the youngest woman ever elected to Kenya's National Assembly, marking a generational shift in county-level representation. In the 13th Parliament she serves on the Delegated Legislation Committee, the Social Protection Committee, and departmental committee work, engaging with subsidiary law-making and social welfare policy alongside her advocacy for Bomet's women and communities.

Chelule Liza Chepkorir is a Kenyan politician who has served as Woman Representative for Nakuru County in the National Assembly across two terms, first elected on a Jubilee Party ticket in the 12th Parliament and re-elected under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) in the 13th Parliament. In the current Parliament she sits on the Administration and Internal Security Committee, the National Cohesion and Equal Opportunity Committee, and the Regional Development Committee, roles through which she engages with security policy, social cohesion, and regional development planning alongside her representation of Nakuru's women and communities.

Lydia Haika Mnene Mizighi is a Kenyan politician who has represented Taita-Taveta County as Woman Representative in the National Assembly across two terms, first elected under the Jubilee Party in the 12th Parliament and re-elected on a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket for the 13th Parliament, taking office on 16 August 2022 for a term running to 2027. In the House she serves on the Diaspora Affairs and Migrant Workers Committee and the Liaison Committee, engaging with policy affecting Kenyans abroad and migrant labor while continuing her advocacy for Taita-Taveta's women and communities.

Betty Maina is a Kenyan politician representing Murang'a County as Woman Representative in the National Assembly, elected on a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket for the 13th Parliament, where she serves on the Public Investments Committee overseeing state corporations and public enterprises. She has also held the position of Cabinet Secretary for Industrialization, Trade and Enterprise Development in the Kenyan government, combining a legislative role representing Murang'a's women and communities with senior executive responsibility for national industrial and trade policy.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Industrialization, Trade and Enterprise Development

bio: Marianne Jebet Kitany is a Kenyan politician representing Aldai Constituency in Nandi County as a Member of the National Assembly, elected on a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket in 2022 with 42,015 votes, unseating incumbent Cornelly Serem. Before entering elective politics, she served as Chief of Staff to Deputy President William Ruto. In the 13th Parliament (2022-2027) she sits on the Public Accounts Committee and the Departmental Committee on Trade, Industry and Cooperatives, roles that draw on her executive branch experience as she engages with government spending oversight and trade policy on behalf of Aldai constituents.

professional.title: Chief of Staff to the Deputy President
professional.institution: Office of the Deputy President of Kenya

bio: Mark Lomunokol is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kacheliba Constituency in the National Assembly across two terms, first elected on a United Republican Party (URP) ticket in the 11th Parliament and re-elected under the Party for Democratic Reform banner in the 12th Parliament. His tenure reflects the frequent party realignments common among West Pokot politicians, as he continued to hold the Kacheliba seat for his constituents through changing political coalitions across both terms in the National Assembly, maintaining a consistent presence in the House representing the region.

Marsden Herman Madoka is a Kenyan statesman, businessman, and former military officer who represented Mwatate Constituency in the National Assembly on a KANU ticket in the 9th Parliament. He held a series of senior government posts, including Assistant Minister for Foreign Affairs, Minister for Foreign Affairs, and Minister of State for Provincial Administration and National Security, giving him responsibility over some of Kenya's most sensitive diplomatic and national security portfolios. Beyond politics and public service, he built a career in business and is recognized for contributions to Kenya's sports development and education sectors, rounding out a career spanning the military, private enterprise, and national government.

professional.title: Minister of State for Provincial Administration and National Security

bio: Martha Wangari Wanjira is a Kenyan politician and Advocate of the High Court of Kenya whose parliamentary career began with her representation of Gichugu Constituency under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) in the 9th Parliament. During that period she served as Minister for Justice until resigning from the position in April 2009, building a reputation for championing women's rights and strengthening Kenya's democratic processes. She later moved constituencies, winning the Gilgil seat in Nakuru County on a Jubilee Party ticket in the 12th Parliament and retaining it for the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) in the 2022 general election with 35,363 votes. In the 13th Parliament she serves on the Defence, Intelligence and Foreign Relations Committee, the Liaison Committee, the Procedure and House Rules Committee, and the Selection Committee, continuing a long record of legislative engagement across two decades.

Martin Nyaga Wambora is a Kenyan politician who served as Member of Parliament for Runyenjes Constituency under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) from 2003 to 2007. Before returning to elective office, he chaired the board of the Kenya Airports Authority, a tenure during which the authority won two of the industry's most prestigious awards in 2011. He went on to become the first Governor of Embu County, winning the seat on a The National Alliance (TNA) ticket in the March 2013 gubernatorial election. Wambora made history as the first Kenyan governor to be impeached, and was impeached a second time, but successfully challenged both removals and was overwhelmingly re-elected to a second term as governor in 2017.

Marubu Muthoni Monicah is a Kenyan politician representing Lamu County as Woman Representative in the National Assembly, having been elected as an independent candidate on 9 August 2022 for the 13th Parliament (2022-2027). In the House she serves on the Departmental Committee on Agriculture and Livestock and the National Cohesion and Equal Opportunity Committee, engaging with agricultural policy and social cohesion issues alongside her advocacy for Lamu's women and communities. Her election as an independent reflects a path to representation built on direct community support rather than party backing.

Mary Emaase Otucho is a Kenyan accountant and politician who represents Teso South Constituency in Busia County in the National Assembly. She was first elected in 2013 on a United Republican Party (URP) ticket, lost her re-election bid in 2017, and returned to Parliament after winning the seat again in the 2022 general election on a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket. In the 13th Parliament (2022-2027) she sits on the Appointments Committee, the Budget and Appropriations Committee, the Departmental Committee on Education, and the Public Accounts Committee, drawing on her accounting background to engage closely with public finance oversight.

Mary Maingi is a Kenyan politician who made history as the first female Member of Parliament for Mwea Constituency in Kirinyaga County, winning the seat in the August 2022 general election on a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket after defeating six other candidates. She is the only woman among Kirinyaga County's four elected constituency Members of Parliament. In the 13th Parliament (2022-2027) she serves on the Departmental Committee on Health and the National Government Constituencies Development Fund Committee, engaging with health policy and the allocation of constituency development resources on behalf of Mwea residents.

Mary Wamaua Waithira Njoroge is a Kenyan politician who represents Maragua Constituency in Murang'a County in the National Assembly, first elected on a Jubilee Party ticket in the 12th Parliament and re-elected under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) banner for the 13th Parliament (2022-2027), now serving her second term. In the House she sits on the Constitutional Implementation Oversight Committee, the Labour Committee, and departmental committee work, engaging with oversight of the constitution's implementation and labour policy while continuing to represent Maragua's constituents in the National Assembly.

Mary Wambui is a Kenyan businesswoman and politician who represented Othaya Constituency in the National Assembly on a The National Alliance (TNA) ticket in the 11th Parliament. Beyond elective politics, she has held senior board leadership roles in Kenya's public sector, serving as Chairperson of the Board of Directors of the Communications Authority of Kenya from 2 December 2022 to 8 August 2025, before taking up her current position as Chairperson of the Board of Directors of the Athi Water Works Development Agency since 8 August 2025. Her career reflects a shift from constituency politics toward regulatory and infrastructure governance roles in Kenya's parastatal sector.

professional.title: Chairperson of the Board of Directors
Chairperson of the Board of Directors

bio: Fatuma Hamisi Masito is a Kenyan politician representing Kwale County as Woman Representative in the National Assembly, elected as a member of the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) for the 13th Parliament (2022-2027). In the House she serves on the Departmental Committee on Environment, Forestry and Mining as well as the Regional Integration Committee, positions through which she engages with environmental and natural resource policy alongside matters of regional and cross-border cooperation. As Woman Representative, she is responsible for bringing the concerns of Kwale's women and communities into national legislative debate.

Mburi Alex Muthengi Muiru is a Kenyan politician who represented Tharaka Constituency in the National Assembly across the 10th and 11th Parliaments, sitting for the United Republican Party (URP) during the 11th Parliament. His time in the House reflected sustained representation of the Tharaka region across consecutive electoral terms, giving constituents continuity in their parliamentary representation over multiple parliamentary sessions and successive general elections spanning more than one term of office in Kenya's National Assembly, a stretch of service that carried him through two distinct electoral cycles.

Mercy Wanjiku Gakuya is a Kenyan politician who represented Kasarani Constituency in Nairobi County as a Member of the National Assembly during the 12th Parliament, elected on a Jubilee Party ticket. Her time in office placed her among the Jubilee Party's cohort of legislators representing Nairobi's densely populated urban constituencies, giving Kasarani a voice in national policy debates throughout her single term in the House during that parliamentary session and general election cycle, a period defined by rapid urban growth across the constituency.

Millie Grace Akoth Odhiambo Mabona is a Kenyan lawyer and politician who has served in the National Assembly since 2008, entering Parliament as a nominated member before being elected to represent Suba North Constituency, a seat she has held for the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) across the 11th, 12th, and 13th Parliaments. In the 13th Parliament (2022-2027) she sits on the Appointments Committee, the Defence, Intelligence and Foreign Relations Committee, the House Business Committee, and the Selection Committee, reflecting a prominent and long-standing role in shaping House business and national security oversight over more than a decade and a half in Parliament.

Mishi Juma Khamisi Mboko is a Kenyan politician who began her parliamentary career as Woman Representative for Mombasa County in the 11th Parliament, before moving to constituency politics as Member of Parliament for Likoni, a seat she has held for the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) since 2017, when she defeated incumbent Masoud Mwahima, and retained in 2022. In the 13th Parliament (2022-2027) she serves on the Diaspora Affairs and Migrant Workers Committee and the Parliamentary Powers and Privileges Committee, engaging with policy affecting Kenyans abroad while continuing her representation of Likoni constituents in the National Assembly.

Mohamed Abdi Kuti is a Kenyan politician who represented Isiolo North Constituency in the National Assembly for ten years, from 2003 to 2013, including a term under the Kenya African National Union (KANU) in the 9th Parliament. He went on to serve a single term as Senator for Isiolo County from 2013 to 2017 as a member of the United Republican Party, before being elected Governor of Isiolo County on 23 August 2017. During his time in national government, he also held cabinet positions as Minister for Youth Affairs in 2005 and Minister for Livestock from 2008 to 2013, giving him a career spanning the National Assembly, the Senate, and county executive leadership.

Mohamed Huka Adan was a Kenyan politician who represented Mandera South Constituency in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament, elected on a United Republican Party (URP) ticket with the backing of the Jubilee Coalition. In the House he served on the Committee on Education, Research and Technology and was an active participant in floor debate, speaking 63 times during the 11th Assembly. He died in March 2019 from an undisclosed illness, ending a single term marked by consistent parliamentary engagement on education policy for Mandera South constituents.

Mohamed Dahir Duale is a Kenyan health professional and politician who has represented Dadaab Constituency in the National Assembly, first elected on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket in the 11th Parliament and later sitting under the Kenya African National Union (KANU) banner in the 12th Parliament. Before entering politics, he worked in various capacities with Kenya's Ministry of Health and the World Health Organization (WHO), building a background in public health that has informed his subsequent legislative career representing Dadaab constituents on health and development matters.

Mohamed Muktar Shidiye is a Kenyan politician who represented Lagdera Constituency in Garissa County across the 8th and 11th Parliaments. He was first elected in 1997 on a Kenya African National Union (KANU) ticket, defeating Farah Maalim, and served as Deputy Government Chief Whip of KANU in Parliament. He lost the seat to Abdillahi Sheikh Dahir in 2002 and to Farah Maalim in 2007, before reclaiming it in 2013 on a TNA ticket with the support of the Jubilee Coalition. Prior to his parliamentary career, he served as a District Officer for Meru District in the early 1990s, giving him grounding in provincial administration before moving into elective politics.

Mohamed Faki Mwinyihaji is a Kenyan politician who represents Mombasa County in the Senate as a member of the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), holding the seat in the 13th Parliament (2022-2027). As Senator, he carries responsibility for representing Mombasa County's interests at the national level, participating in the Senate's role of safeguarding county government functions and interests within Kenya's devolved system of government at the coast, a role central to the Senate's oversight mandate over county affairs and resource allocation.

Moses Otieno Kajwang' is a Kenyan politician who has served as Senator for Homa Bay County since 2015, becoming the second person to hold the seat when he won a by-election on 12 February 2015 following the death of his brother, Gerald Otieno Kajwang'. Standing for the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), he was re-elected for a second term in 2017 and a third term in 2022, giving him one of the longest continuous tenures among Kenya's senators. His decade-long representation of Homa Bay County reflects sustained voter confidence across three consecutive election cycles.

Moses K. Lessonet is a Kenyan politician and accountant who has represented Eldama Ravine Constituency in the National Assembly across the 10th, 11th, and 12th Parliaments. He was first elected in the 2007 general election on an Orange Democratic Movement ticket, later contesting successfully in 2013 as a member of the United Republican Party and again in 2017 under the Jubilee Party. Trained as an accountant, he graduated from the University of Nairobi in 1993 with a Bachelor of Commerce degree in Accounting, a professional background that has informed his engagement with public finance matters throughout more than a decade representing Eldama Ravine in Kenya's Parliament.

Moses Somoine ole Sakuda is a Kenyan politician who entered the National Assembly after winning a September 2012 by-election on a TNA ticket, before going on to represent Kajiado West Constituency in the 11th Parliament, where he served on departmental committee work. His path to Parliament through a special by-election reflects the fluid nature of Kajiado's constituency politics in the years surrounding Kenya's 2013 general election, and his subsequent tenure gave the region continued representation in the House during that period.

Moses Francis Masika Wetang'ula is a Kenyan politician who has held seats across multiple levels of Kenya's Parliament, beginning as Member of Parliament for Sirisia Constituency under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) in the 9th Parliament, and later serving as Senator for Bungoma County under FORD-Kenya, a party he leads as one of Western Kenya's most influential political forces. He was elected Speaker of the National Assembly on 8 September 2022, a position he continues to hold in the 13th Parliament, where he also engages with the Appointments Committee, House Business Committee, Parliamentary Pensions Management Committee, Parliamentary Powers and Privileges Committee, and Procedure and House Rules Committee. Earlier in his career he served as Kenya's Minister for Foreign Affairs from 2008 to 2010 and again from 2011 to 2012, and as Minister for Trade from 2012 to 2013.

Dr. Mukhisa Kituyi is a Kenyan politician and economist who represented Kimilili Constituency in the National Assembly under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) in the 9th Parliament, during which he served as Kenya's Minister of Trade and Industry from 2002 to 2007. He later became Chief Executive of the Kenya Institute of Governance in Nairobi before his appointment as Secretary-General of the United Nations Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD), a post he held from September 2013 until February 2021, when he stepped down to run for the Kenyan presidency. He dropped that presidential bid in March 2022 to support former Prime Minister Raila Odinga, capping a career spanning national legislative politics, cabinet government, and international diplomacy.

professional.title: Secretary-General
professional.institution: United Nations Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD)

bio: Musa Cherutich Sirma is a Kenyan politician who has represented Eldama Ravine Constituency in the National Assembly across the 8th, 9th, and 13th Parliaments, including an earlier term under the Kenya African National Union (KANU) and a later return on a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket. Between his parliamentary terms, he served as Kenya's Minister for East African and Regional Cooperation, a cabinet role focused on regional integration. In the 13th Parliament (2022-2027) he sits on the Liaison Committee, the National Assembly Select Committee, and the National Government Constituencies Development Fund Committee, continuing a legislative career that spans several decades of representing Eldama Ravine.

professional.title: Minister for East African and Regional Cooperation

bio: Mutahi Kagwe is a Kenyan politician who represented Mukurweini Constituency in the National Assembly under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) in the 9th Parliament. He later returned to national government as Cabinet Secretary for Health, assuming office on 28 February 2020, a tenure that placed him at the center of Kenya's response to the COVID-19 pandemic following the country's first confirmed case in Nairobi on 12 March 2020. He currently serves as Cabinet Secretary for Agriculture and Livestock Development, extending a career that has moved from constituency representation to senior public health and agricultural policy leadership at the national level.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Agriculture and Livestock Development

bio: Mutava Musyimi is a Kenyan politician who has represented the Mbeere South area in the National Assembly since the 2007 general election, having first won the Gachoka Constituency seat under the Party of National Unity before continuing to serve the area on The National Alliance (TNA) ticket in the 11th Parliament. In the House he sat on the Budget and Appropriations Committee, the Mediation Committee, and departmental committee work, giving him a role in national fiscal oversight and inter-house dispute resolution alongside his long-standing representation of the constituency and its residents.

Mutavi Charles Kilonzo is a Kenyan politician who has represented Yatta Constituency in the National Assembly since the 2007 general election, having been first elected as a member of the Orange Democratic Movement-Kenya (ODM-Kenya) before continuing to hold the seat as an independent candidate in the 12th Parliament. His sustained representation of Yatta across multiple electoral cycles and shifting political affiliations reflects a durable base of support among constituents in Machakos County over more than a decade of service in the National Assembly.

Onesmus Muthomi Njuki is a Kenyan politician who represented Chuka/Igambang'ombe Constituency in the National Assembly from 2013 to 2017 as a member of the Alliance Party of Kenya, serving on the Mediation Committee during the 11th Parliament. He went on to become the second Governor of Tharaka Nithi County, winning election on a Jubilee Party ticket in the 2017 general election and continuing as the county's incumbent governor. His career reflects a progression from constituency-level legislative representation to county executive leadership in Tharaka Nithi County.

Emilio Stanley Mwai Kibaki was a Kenyan politician who became the longest-serving Member of Parliament in Kenya's history, representing Othaya under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) and earlier parties from 1963 to 2013. He served as the fourth Vice-President of Kenya from 1978 to 1988 under President Daniel arap Moi, and held cabinet posts including Minister for Finance from 1969 to 1981, Minister for Home Affairs from 1982 to 1988, and Minister for Health from 1988 to 1991. After leading the official opposition in Parliament from 1998 to 2002, following unsuccessful presidential bids in 1992 and 1997, he was elected Kenya's third President in December 2002, serving until April 2013. His presidency introduced free primary education in 2003 and a new constitution in 2010, though it was also marked by the disputed 2007 election and the violence that followed.

Mwalimu Masudi Mwahima was a Kenyan politician who represented Likoni Constituency in the National Assembly as a member of the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), having first been elected in the 2007 Kenyan general election and continuing to hold the seat into the 11th Parliament. His decade in office gave Likoni sustained representation within a single party through successive election cycles, before he was ultimately succeeded by a new generation of ODM politicians representing the Mombasa County constituency in subsequent parliaments.

Festus Mwangi Kiunjuri is a Kenyan politician born in Kieni, Nyeri County, who has represented Laikipia East Constituency in the National Assembly across two Parliaments, first under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) in the 9th Parliament and later returning on a The Service Party ticket in the 13th Parliament. In his current term he sits on the Departmental Committee on Transport and Infrastructure, the Public Investments Committee, and the Parliamentary Powers and Privileges Committee, reflecting a broad legislative portfolio spanning infrastructure oversight, state enterprise scrutiny, and parliamentary conduct. Over the course of his career he has held various other political and government positions beyond his constituency role.

Mwinga Gunga Chea was a Kenyan lawyer and politician who represented Kaloleni Constituency in Kilifi County in the National Assembly, elected in 2013 on the ticket of the Kenya African Democratic Union-Asili (KADU-Asili) party for the 11th Parliament. His legal background informed his single term of legislative service representing Kaloleni's constituents before his death in 2020, closing out a career that combined the practice of law with elected office at the coast of Kenya, where he was known for his advocacy on behalf of the constituency.

Najib Mohamed Balala is a Kenyan politician of Hadhrami Yemeni ancestry who represented Mvita Constituency in the National Assembly under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) in the 9th Parliament. He went on to lead the Republican Congress Party of Kenya (RC), a partner within the Jubilee Coalition, and later served as Kenya's Cabinet Secretary for Tourism, a role in which he oversaw national tourism policy at the executive level. His career reflects a path from coastal constituency politics through party leadership to senior cabinet responsibility for one of Kenya's key economic sectors.

Naomi Namisi Shaban is a Kenyan politician from the border town of Taveta who became the first woman elected to represent Taveta Constituency in Parliament, winning the seat in 2002 on a Kenya African National Union (KANU) ticket and successfully defending it in 2007 under the same party. She later joined The National Alliance (TNA) and was re-elected to the National Assembly in 2013, then secured a fourth term in 2017 in a closely contested and controversial election, with opponent Morris Mutiso alleging irregularities and seeking annulment of the results. Her long tenure representing Taveta across the 9th, 11th, and 12th Parliaments makes her one of the constituency's most enduring political figures and a pioneering woman in Kenyan parliamentary politics.

Nelson Gaichuhie is a Kenyan politician who has represented Subukia Constituency in the National Assembly since the 2007 general election, continuing to hold the seat as a member of The National Alliance (TNA) in the 11th Parliament. In the House he took part in departmental committee work, including contributing to departmental committee reports, over the course of his tenure representing Subukia's constituents across successive electoral cycles and legislative sessions in Kenya's Parliament and the wider Rift Valley region of Nakuru County.

Newton Wanjala Kulundu was a Kenyan politician who represented Lurambi Constituency in the National Assembly from 1997 to 2007, including a term under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) in the 9th Parliament. In government he served as Minister of Environment and Natural Resources from 2003 to 2005, and later as Minister for Labour and Human Resource Development from 2005 to 2008, giving him responsibility over both environmental policy and national labour affairs during a decade-long career in Kenya's cabinet and national legislature.

professional.title: Minister of Environment and Natural Resources
Minister for Labour and Human Resource Development

bio: Nimrod Mbithuka Mbai is a Kenyan politician who represents Kitui East Constituency in Kitui County as a Member of the National Assembly, first elected on a Jubilee Party ticket in the 12th Parliament and re-elected under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) banner for the 13th Parliament (2022-2027). In the House he sits on the Constitutional Implementation Oversight Committee and the Public Petitions Committee, engaging with oversight of the constitution's implementation and the handling of public grievances submitted to Parliament, alongside earlier departmental committee work in the 12th Parliament. He remains an incumbent expected to defend his Kitui East seat in the 2027 general election.

Norman M.G.K. Nyagah is a Kenyan politician, the son of Jeremiah Nyagah and brother of former presidential candidate Joseph Nyagah, who built a parliamentary career spanning a decade in the National Assembly. He represented Gachoka Constituency from 1997 to 2002 before moving to represent Kamukunji Constituency from 2002 to 2007 under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC), during which he also served as chief whip in Parliament. Ahead of the 2007 general election he lost the ruling Party of National Unity's nomination contest to Simon Mbugua and subsequently defected to The Independent Party of Kenya (TIP), a small party led by Kalembe Ndile, bringing his time in national politics to a turbulent close.

Winfred Nyiva Mwendwa is a Kenyan politician who made history as the first Kenyan woman to serve as a cabinet minister. She represented Kitui West Constituency in the National Assembly under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) in the 9th Parliament, and later returned to national politics as Woman Representative for Kitui County under the Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya (WDM-K) in the 11th Parliament. Her career bridges constituency-based representation and county-level Woman Representative office, anchored by her pioneering role as a trailblazer for women in Kenya's executive government.

Oburu Ng'ong'a Oginga is a Kenyan politician who has represented Siaya County in the Senate since 8 August 2022 as a member of the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM). Earlier in his career he represented Bondo Constituency in the National Assembly under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) in the 9th Parliament, and was later a nominated Member of Parliament for ODM in the 11th Parliament. He served as Assistant Minister for Finance in the 2008 Grand Coalition Government, and between 2017 and 2022 he was a member of the East African Legislative Assembly, giving him experience at the national, regional, and East African Community levels of representation before his election to the Senate.

Ochilo George Mbogo Ayacko is a Kenyan politician who represented Rongo Constituency in the National Assembly under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) in the 9th Parliament. He later moved into county leadership, serving as Governor of Migori County since August 2022, a role in which he oversees devolved government functions for the county. His career reflects a progression from national legislative representation to executive leadership at the county level following Kenya's shift to devolved governance under the 2010 constitution, spanning both chambers of Kenyan public office.

Catherine Nakhabi Omanyo is a Kenyan politician, education activist, and human rights advocate representing Busia County as Woman Representative in the National Assembly, elected on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket for the 13th Parliament. She is known for her advocacy on women's rights and for expanding access to education for poor and orphaned children, work she has carried out as founder of the International School for Champions, a tuition-free school located in Busia County near the border with Uganda. In the House she serves on the Public Investments Committee, the Special Funds Account Committee, and the Sports and Culture Committee, extending her activism into legislative oversight of public spending and cultural affairs.

Omar Mwinyi Shimbwa is a Kenyan politician who has represented Changamwe Constituency in Mombasa County in the National Assembly for the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) since 2013, having been re-elected in both 2017 and 2022. In the 13th Parliament he sits on the Departmental Committee on Lands and the Procedure and House Rules Committee, engaging with land policy and House procedural matters. His decade-plus tenure across the 11th, 12th, and 13th Parliaments reflects sustained voter support for his representation of Changamwe's constituents in Mombasa County.

Onesmas Kimani Ngunjiri is a Kenyan politician who represented Bahati Constituency in Nakuru County in the National Assembly across two terms, first elected under The National Alliance (TNA) in the 11th Parliament and later re-elected as a member of the Jubilee Party in the 12th Parliament. His continued hold on the Bahati seat across both terms reflected sustained support from constituents through the transition between these two allied political parties in Kenya's changing political landscape at the time, spanning two consecutive general elections.
